spec_match.rationale: Touches rtpTimestamp from the chromestatus summary but skips VideoFrame.metadata, webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry, dom-videoframemetadata — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, canvas); controls include "Start synthetic stream"; "Stop"; "Inject network spike" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link found — the self-heal cleanup script cannot recover this folder if the slug ever drifts.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on jitter-tracker
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Expose rtpTimestamp from WebRTC video frames via VideoFrame.metadata() introduces VideoFrame.metadata, webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry, dom-videoframemetadata but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://www.w3.org/TR/webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry/#dom-videoframemetadata

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on jitter-tracker
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link to the see-also section
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #3: every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link so the slug-recovery script can recover this folder if the name ever drifts.

summary: jitter-tracker covers the happy path of Expose rtpTimestamp from WebRTC video frames via VideoFrame.metadata() and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
